commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But you shall die in peace: and with the ceremonial fires of your fathers, the former kings who were before you, so shall they burn incense for you; and they will lament you, saying, Alas lord. for I have pronounced the word, says the LORD. That is, by a natural death. (Challoner) Burn thee, like Asa; (2 Paralipomenon xvi. ; Calmet) or aromatical spices were only burnt over their dead bodies, which were also embalmed. (Sanctius; Vatable) To stat says that Sedecias was intoxicated, for sport, (Habacuc ii. ) and died of grief in prison, chap. (Calmet) The king of Babylon would probably not refuse him a decent burial. (Watson, Let.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
